title: betula incana
intro: Betula incana, also known as gray birch, is a deciduous tree or shrub species native to the boreal forests of northern North America. The bark of Betula incana contains salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a valuable medicinal plant. The health benefits of Betula incana include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. Betula incana can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
